MCX – SEBI EASE OF DOING BUSINESS PROPOSALS 📑⚖️

• Multi Commodity Exchange of India impacted by SEBI consultation paper on simplifying exchange-traded derivatives regulations

• SEBI proposes merging multiple master circulars for stock exchanges and commodity derivatives segments

• Aim is to remove duplicate and obsolete compliance requirements across exchanges and clearing corporations

• Proposal to remove “Close To Money (CTM)” option series norms in commodity derivatives for simpler exercise mechanism

• Exchanges may receive greater flexibility in monitoring position limits and operational compliance

• Product Advisory Committee (PAC) meeting frequency for non-agri commodities may be reduced to once a year

• Daily newspaper/media disclosure requirements for derivatives transactions may be discontinued; exchange website disclosure to continue

• Compliance framework for trading software, surveillance, risk management, and clearing corporations to be streamlined

• Overall objective is to reduce compliance burden and improve ease of doing business for market infrastructure institutions

• 🔄 Proposal includes merging multiple master circulars and removing duplicate / obsolete compliance requirements

• 📉 SEBI proposes removing “Close To Money (CTM)” option series norms in commodity derivatives to simplify exercise mechanism

• 📊 Exchanges may get more flexibility in monitoring position limits and operational compliance responsibilities

• 🧾 Proposal to reduce mandatory Product Advisory Committee (PAC) meeting frequency for non-agri commodities to once a year

• 🌐 Daily disclosure requirements through newspapers/media for derivatives transactions proposed to be discontinued; exchange website disclosure may continue

• ⚙️ Several compliance provisions related to clearing corporations, trading software, surveillance, risk management and commodity derivatives proposed to be streamlined

• 🏢 Aim is to improve ease of doing business, reduce duplication and lower compliance burden on exchanges & market participants

🟢 Impact: Positive for Exchanges & Market Infrastructure Institutions (Lower compliance burden, operational simplification, improved regulatory efficiency)

JPM on Kaynes
Downgrade to N, TP cut to Rs 4000

Missed its own 4QFY27 revenue guidance by 27% & even missed lowered Street/JPM expectations by 18%/13%.

Net working capital days remained elevated at 125 days vs. guidance of 85 days

Cutting earnings by 12-17% over the next two years led by cuts across core EMS, OSAT and PCB businesses

Cutting core EMS multiple to 33x from 45x led by

1) a cut in revenue growth over the next two years as well over the medium to long term in our DCF; and

2) an increase in NWC days over the medium term in our DCF.
While still expect strong 40%/45% revenue/earnings CAGR over FY26-28E thanks to the ramp up of OSAT and PCB businesses, we believe the stock will remain a ‘show me’ stock until the gap between actual numbers and company guidance narrows.

CLSA on Kanes
O-P, TP Rs 4200

Expect stock to react negatively to its 4Q results, which missed company guidance & street expectations on operational parameters

Its balance sheet also deteriorated further, which was a key item heading into its results.
4Q revenue grew 26% YoY to Rs12.4bn (CLSA: Rs15.7bn; company guidance: Rs17bn) while its margin fell 150bps YoY to 15.6% (CLSA: 16.5%)

Calculate working capital increased 85 days YoY (2Q: 116 days) while net debt was at Rs711mn (FY25 net debt: Rs1.9bn).

Sugar Stocks in Focus

The Central Government bans the export of sugar with immediate effect till September 30, 2026, or until further orders. Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) issues a notification amending the export policy from 'Restricted' to 'Prohibited'.

The prohibition will not apply to sugar exports to the EU and USA under CXL and TRQ quota, the Advance Authorisation Scheme (AAS), and Government-to-Government shipments to meet food security needs of other countries. Consignments already in the physical export pipeline are also exempted.

Delhi government has started a special campaign to find and bring back nearly 19,000 HIV patients who stopped their treatment.

Quality Power (QPOWER) - Q4 FY26

Q4 EBITDA margin dipped to 21.1%, below the 22% floor. Before you react, read what caused it.

Q4 numbers

Q4 revenue: ₹281 Cr, up 159.3% YoY and down 1.1% QoQ - flat sequentially, which is fine. 

Q4 EBITDA margin: 21.1% vs Q3's 27.9%, a 680 bps sequential drop. Q4 normalized PAT: ₹73 Cr, +139% YoY. The dip was almost entirely one item.

The Q4 margin: read the footnote

Endoks Enerji (Turkey subsidiary) recorded a ₹25.7 Cr hyperinflation loss under IndAS 29 in Q4. This is classified under Other Expenses - which is why Other Expenses surged 234% QoQ (₹17.6 Cr → ₹58.9 Cr). Hyperinflation accounting is a non-cash monetary asset adjustment for operating in a hyperinflationary economy. 

Endoks itself runs at ~30% operating margins. The business is healthy - Turkey macro is the issue, not the operations. Separately, material costs ticked up 210 bps as a percentage of Q4 revenue, commodity input pressure that is real and worth tracking. These two together explain the Q4 miss.

FY26 in context

Full year: revenue ₹947 Cr vs guided ₹700–850 Cr - beat the upper end by ₹97 Cr. FY26 EBITDA margin: 24.9% - above the 22% floor. 

Mehru EBITDA margin reached ~20% in Q4, ahead of the 16–17% target management set at acquisition. Normalized PAT FY26: ₹207 Cr, +107% YoY. Every metric beat guidance on a full-year basis. The Q4 dip is noise against a strong year.

Working capital: the one real concern

CFO/PAT FY26: 0.43x. Trade receivables absorbed ₹106 Cr, 40% of operating cash flow. Inventory built ₹14 Cr. This is project-execution working capital - milestone billing creates lags. The balance sheet is clean (D/E 4.6%), current ratio 2.49x, so there's no stress. But cash conversion needs to improve in FY27.

Guidance scorecard:

Revenue ₹700–850 Cr → ₹947 Cr ✅
EBITDA margin floor 22% → FY26: 24.9% ✅ · Q4: 21.1% ⚠
Mehru margin 16–17% → ~20% ✅
Sangli plant June 2026 → August 2026 ❌
CFO/PAT → 0.43x ⚠

What to watch in FY27: Does Q4's margin recover above 22% in Q1 as hyperinflation accounting normalizes? Does Sangli August commissioning hold without further slippage? And do receivables finally convert or does the working capital drag persist?

Note: This is not investment advice.

Valuation Gravity: India’s Global Footprint Erodes

India’s share in global market capitalization has cratered to 2.9% following a brutal $180 billion rout over just two sessions. This retreat from a peak of 4.73% marks a four-year low and highlights a significant drawdown in investor confidence as the domestic mcap settles at $4.77 trillion. While the broader market remains the world's fifth-largest, the rapid deterioration suggests that the aggressive re-rating seen since 2020 has finally collided with fiscal reality.

The "friction point" is the aggressive cannibalization of market share by regional peers; Taiwan and South Korea are poised to overtake India following massive rallies of 45% and 75% respectively. This shift underscores a pivot away from India's expensive valuations and toward markets with heavy AI-linked exposure. The lack of large-scale AI plays and vulnerability to energy shocks have dulled the alpha, transforming a "clean" growth story into a liquidity trap for over-leveraged bulls.

BOTTOM LINE:
India’s mcap dominance has slipped into a dangerous trendline as regional competitors weaponize AI-linked growth. Failure to justify premium valuations against a -8.5% YTD performance risks a prolonged institutional exit toward more competitive tech-heavy markets.

text:
BHARTI AIRTEL TO INCREASE STAKE IN AIRTEL AFRICA VIA ₹28,220 CR SHARE SWAP 🌍📡

• 📈 Board approves issuance of up to 14.67 Cr equity shares to Indian Continent Investment Ltd (ICIL) at ₹1,923/share on preferential basis

• 🔄 Airtel to acquire up to 16.31% stake (59.52 Cr shares) in Airtel Africa from ICIL through share swap deal

• 💰 Transaction valued at up to ~₹28,220 Cr (GBP 3.659/share for Airtel Africa)

• 🌍 Airtel Africa is a UK-listed subsidiary; Airtel currently holds 62.73% stake through Airtel Africa Mauritius

• 📊 Deal expected to strengthen Airtel’s control in strategic African telecom operations

• 💸 Transaction is cashless & leverage neutral; management says deal will be EPS accretive for Airtel India

• 📈 Airtel India shares proposed at ~9.5% premium to last closing price before relevant date

• 📉 Corresponding Airtel Africa acquisition at ~11.6% discount to last closing price before relevant date

• 🏛️ Deal subject to shareholder & regulatory approvals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Higher stake in fast-growing Africa business, EPS accretive structure & stronger long-term telecom positioning)

The B2B Engine: India’s Silent Digital Arbitrage

Indian B2B e-commerce is scaling toward a USD 2.5 trillion market by FY30, yet digital penetration currently sits at a staggering sub-2%. This isn't just organic growth; it's a structural pivot where full-stack procurement platforms are capturing massive whitespaces. As institutional flows chase high-velocity QComm growth of 100% YoY, the real alpha is accumulating in these heavy-duty B2B channels that dwarf consumer-facing GMV.

The "friction point" lies in the disconnect between valuation hype and real-world adoption. While New-age logistics faces distress-driven consolidation, B2B e-commerce remains largely uncrowded despite its massive headroom. The common narrative focuses on the 270 million shoppers in consumer retail, but the industrial digital shift is where the unit economics are actually stabilizing, moving away from volume-at-all-costs toward disciplined pricing.

BOTTOM LINE:
The 2% digital penetration in a multi-trillion dollar B2B market represents the single largest structural trade in the region. Ignore the high-burn consumer noise; the institutional play is in the industrial digitisation and logistics consolidation.

text:
WABAG & PEAK ANNOUNCE FIRST BIO-CNG PROJECT IN GHAZIABAD ♻️⛽

• 🌱 VA Tech WABAG and PEAK Sustainability Ventures to develop first Bio-CNG plant in Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh

• 🏭 Project to be set up at 70 MLD Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) in Dundahera under BOT model on PPP basis

• 🤝 Project to be executed through newly formed JV entity — Ghaziabad Bioenergy Pvt Ltd

• 🔥 Plant will capture raw biogas from STP and upgrade it into renewable Bio-CNG fuel for mobility & industrial use

• 🌍 Estimated carbon emission reduction of ~2.5 lakh metric tons over project life

• ⏳ Commercial operations expected to commence within one year

• 🛠️ WABAG to handle project execution plus O&M for 15 years

• 🇮🇳 Major equipment to be sourced domestically, supporting Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens WABAG’s presence in waste-to-energy & green fuel segment with long-term revenue and sustainability benefits)

Bajel Projects Limited has been awarded a major EPC order for a 400kV transmission line under the Bellary–Davanagere RE evacuation scheme by PowerGrid Corporation of India Limited (PGCIL). ⚡

𝗞𝗲𝘆 𝗗𝗲𝘁𝗮𝗶𝗹𝘀:

- 𝗢𝗿𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝗩𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗲: Classified as a 'Major Order', typically falling between ₹200 Cr to ₹300 Cr as per company policy (inclusive of GST).

- 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁 𝗦𝗰𝗼𝗽𝗲: Construction of a ~70 km, 400kV (Quad) Double Circuit transmission line connecting Bellary Substation to Davanagere Substation. This is crucial for evacuating renewable power.

- 𝗔𝘄𝗮𝗿𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗘𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘁𝘆: PowerGrid Corporation of India Limited (PGCIL), on behalf of their SPV M/s Bellary Davanagere Power Transmission Limited

- 𝗡𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘁: EPC Contract for Transmission Line works.

- 𝗧𝗶𝗺𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲: 21 Months from the date of issuance of the Notification of Award. ⏳

- 𝗕𝘂𝘀𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝗔𝗹𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁: The order is in the ordinary course of business & aligns with India's energy transition priorities, strengthening the national grid for renewable energy integration.

PASUPATI ACRYLON GETS APPROVAL TO EXPAND ETHANOL CAPACITY 

•  Company received consent from Excise Department, Uttar Pradesh Government to enhance ethanol plant capacity

• Ethanol manufacturing capacity to increase from 150 KL/day to 180 KL/day

•  Existing plant operating at 100% utilization, subject to allocation orders from Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs)

•  Capacity expansion expected to be completed in Q1 FY27

•  No additional investment required for the expansion

•  Capacity enhancement aimed at improving operational efficiency

HPCL Says: 

Q1 is expected to be tough and loss making the company.

Crude supplies are secured till July and they typically maintain around 2 months of secured crude availability at any point.

Given the volatility, no forward guidance is being issued right now.

Sourcing patterns have also shifted. In normal times, around 50% of crude sourcing comes from term contracts and the rest from spot markets. Since the crisis began, dependence on spot contracts has increased significantly.

A substantial amount of Russian crude was also sourced during the period as companies tried to manage losses and secure supplies

They have also sourced crude from Venezuela and US.

Crude has not disappeared from the market, there are multiple avenues available

Add enough LPG supplies are available

Forex Shields Under Fire

India’s forex reserves have shrunk by 5.2% since March 2026, dropping from $728.5 billion to $690.7 billion by May 1. As crude prices surge, the RBI has burned through billions to defend a rupee that recently touched a life-time low of 95.62 against the dollar. This depletion is among the highest for oil-importing emerging markets, leaving the currency increasingly vulnerable.

While other EMs like Hungary and Chile have managed to conserve or even grow their resources, India faces a double whammy of capital outflows and high import costs. Even with a brief recovery in April, sustained pressure in the final weeks has forced the central bank to tighten curbs on banks' dollar exposure. The lesson? A massive war chest provides protection, but even the deepest pockets have limits when geopolitical shocks hit the energy basket this hard.

HPCL - Q4 FY26

PAT ₹18,047 Cr in FY26 that's 2.7x FY25. Debt down ₹15,588 Cr. FCF ₹28,156 Cr. The ₹40,000 Cr EBITDA FY28 target is at 76% with two years left.

Q4 numbers

Q4 PAT: ₹6,065 Cr (+77.6% YoY). EBITDA margin: 7.22% (+248 bps YoY). Revenue from operations: ₹1,14,937 Cr (+4.8% YoY) - volume-led with domestic sales up 2.6%. The two drivers of outperformance: GRM improvement and JV/Associate turnaround.

GRM: the structural story

FY26 GRM: $8.79/bbl vs $5.74/bbl in FY25 - a 53% improvement. Management guided $3–4/bbl benefit from VRMP (Visakh Refinery Modernisation Project). The actual delta vs FY25 was +$3.05/bbl - confirmed. VRMP's RUF (Residue Upgradation Facility) is mechanically complete and commissioning underway, full benefits are expected to flow through in FY27.

JV/Associate: from drag to driver

Q4 JV/Associate profit: ₹1,290 Cr vs ₹349 Cr in Q4 FY25 (+270% YoY). FY26: ₹1,493 Cr vs FY25 loss of ₹120 Cr. HMEL (Bhatinda refinery) recovery and early HRRL contributions drove this swing. A ₹1,613 Cr turnaround in associate profitability YoY added directly to consolidated PAT.

Balance sheet: transformed

D/E: 0.78x vs 1.30x a year ago. Gross debt reduced from ₹63,323 Cr to ₹47,599 Cr, ₹15,724 Cr of deleveraging in one year. Interest service coverage improved from 4.05x to 8.19x. CFO/PAT: 2.1x - strong cash conversion. FCF: ₹28,156 Cr. Dividend paid: ₹3,298 Cr (₹24.25/share total for FY26).

The cost headwinds are structural, not a concern

Finance costs +34.8% YoY in Q4 - higher short-term borrowings and rates. This should ease as deleveraging continues. Depreciation +51.3% YoY - VRMP and new assets coming onstream create a permanent step-up in D&A. That's the cost of the capacity being built, not operational deterioration. The ₹2,491 Cr forex loss in FY26 is the more uncomfortable item - rupee volatility directly hits P&L and management cannot control it.

The ₹40,000 Cr EBITDA path

FY26 standalone EBITDA: ₹30,491 Cr. Target: ₹40,000 Cr by FY28. Gap: ₹9,509 Cr over two years. The math: Barmer (HRRL) refinery is 89% complete, crude-in expected in FY27 - management projects ₹5,000–5,500 Cr EBITDA contribution. VRMP full benefits add another ₹1,000–2,000 Cr. Organic growth covers the rest. The target is achievable, it's not a stretch if execution holds.

Guidance scorecard:

EBITDA ₹40,000 Cr by FY28 → ₹30,491 Cr · on track ✅
Monthly PAT ₹1,100–1,300 Cr → ₹1,634 Cr/month · exceeded ✅
VRMP GRM benefit $3–4/bbl → +$3.05/bbl · confirmed ✅
LPG compensation ₹3,300 Cr → recognized in full ✅
GRM mid-cycle $20/bbl → $8.79/bbl · cyclical gap ⚠️

What to watch in FY27: Does Barmer crude-in happen as guided and does the ₹5,000 Cr EBITDA contribution materialize on schedule? Does the forex loss moderate, or does currency volatility remain a persistent P&L drag?

Note: This is not investment advice.

Manufacturing will be a larger part of benchmarks going forward as it's one of the few pockets of the market where growth is broad-based and secular.

As an example, TCS grew revenues by INR 41,563 cr during FY23-26. During that same period, Dixon grew revenues by INR 36,681 cr. Think about it - Dixon's absolute revenue accretion in the last 3 years was ~88% of the country's largest IT services business even though it's FY23 revenue was ~5% of TCS FY23 revenue. 

Nifty 50 has negligible exposure to manufacturing businesses. We doubt that this will still be the case a decade from now.

When returns are good, almost anyone can invest!

Just look at the SIP ageing data across different periods.

Between March 2022 and March 2023, markets were rallying and Direct Plan SIPs (137% growth) were growing faster than Regular Plan SIPs (63% growth).

Leverage is pro-cyclical i.e., increase as the markets go up, which has been the case in the post-pandemic period. While leverage in F&O has steadily reduced because of regulatory tightening, some of it has migrated to margin trading facility (MTF).

MTF's popularity is very recent and most retail customers don't really understand the risks. While we offer MTF, we do it without being loud about it or constantly pushing it. We are probably the only platform to warn users before they enable MTF.

Hike in import duty on gold will be +ve for Gold NBFCs

Why?
1. The collateral value will increase for Gold NBFCs. When the duty rises, the domestic price of gold jumps increases. This increases the value of the gold already sitting in the vaults of NBFCs

2. Loan-to-Value (LTV) Headroom: Higher prices lower the LTV ratio for existing loans. For example, if a customer borrowed ₹75,000 against ₹1,00,000 worth of gold, and the gold value suddenly jumps to ₹1,10,000 due to duty hikes, the lender's risk decreases significantly.

text:
Patil Automation Limited – H2 & 12M FY26 Concall Highlights

Strong business momentum backed by healthy execution, expansion plans and rising demand for industrial automation solutions across key sectors.

📌 Key Highlights:

- H2 FY26 PAT grew 49% YoY
- Margins continued to improve steadily
- Management guiding FY27 revenue at ₹260–270 Cr
- FY28 revenue target of ₹380–385 Cr with further expansion plans
- Current peak capacity estimated at ₹260–300 Cr
- Further CAPEX discussions already underway for next phase of growth
- Strong positioning across Auto, EV, Defence & Renewable sectors
- Focus on Industry 4.0, Smart Factory & IoT-enabled automation solutions
- Predominantly indigenous sourcing reduces geopolitical/raw material risks
- Technology-driven business model supports healthy profitability

Park Medi World Limited delivered a strong performance in FY26, marked by consistent growth and improved financial strength. The company reported a 20.5% year-on-year increase in consolidated revenue to ₹16,793.56 Mn, while PAT grew by 27% to ₹2,735.57 Mn, reflecting enhanced profitability and operating efficiency.

In Q4 FY26, momentum further accelerated, with revenue rising 30.1% YoY and EBITDA and PAT growing by 44% and 46.6% respectively, supported by margin expansion.

The year was also defined by strategic milestones, including a successful IPO, aggressive acquisitions, and significant deleveraging, reducing debt by 95%. Strong operating cash flows and capacity expansion initiatives position the company well for sustained growth, reinforcing its trajectory as a scalable healthcare platform.

Global Copper Supply-Demand Model

Copper Supply and Demand Outlook

The copper market is expected to enter a period of growing deficits due to significant supply constraints and increasing global demand. 

Jefferies' supply and demand model forecasts that total global copper demand will grow at a CAGR of 2.1% from 2025 to 2030E, reaching 30,931 kt by 2030, while total supply is projected to grow at a slower CAGR of 1.7%, reaching 30,136 kt in the same period. 

This imbalance is expected to result in a widening deficit, with a notional deficit of (795 kt) projected by 2030E.

Deep dive on the fiber opportunity

Global fiber production is around 570 million fiber-kilometres annually, with APAC dominating output, but US hyperscalers increasingly require domestically produced fiber due to CHIPS Act incentives, geopolitical risks, and supply chain audits. 

Projects like Meta’s Hyperion data center in Louisiana require 12.9 million fkm of fiber, highlighting the scale of demand, with estimates suggesting half of US fiber capacity may be needed for AI infrastructure by 2027.

**THE HIDDEN INFRASTRUCTURE LAYER BEHIND AI, 5G, EVs & INDUSTRIAL AUTOMATION ⚡️📡🤖**

**The Under-Tracked Opportunity**
- One of the most overlooked infrastructure layers behind:
  - AI data centres  
  - 5G telecom rollout  
  - EV ecosystem  
  - Industrial automation  
  - Defence electronics  
  - Semiconductor manufacturing  

- Core enabler is:
  - Programmable AC/DC power systems  
  - Industrial testing infrastructure  
  - Precision validation equipment  

- Global rack-mount power supply market estimated at ~$8.6Bn in 2025 and projected to exceed ~$12Bn by 2030  

---

**1. AI Data Centres & Hyperscale Compute – Largest Demand Driver**
- AI hyperscale deployments reportedly contribute nearly 55% of industry demand  
- Every AI server rack and GPU cluster requires:
  - PSU validation  
  - Harmonic analysis  
  - Surge simulation  
  - Frequency testing  
  - Battery backup testing  
  - Rack power reliability checks  

- Modern AI infrastructure depends on:
  - N+1 redundancy  
  - Hot-swappable power modules  
  - Precision voltage regulation  
  - Remote monitoring systems  

- AI-related programmable power demand reportedly growing at ~11%+ CAGR globally  
- Hidden opportunity lies not only in GPUs and servers, but in the testing and power ecosystem enabling stable deployments  

---

**2. Telecom & 5G Infrastructure**
- Telecom and 5G contribute nearly 18% industry share  
- Massive testing opportunity emerging across:
  - BTS racks  
  - Base stations  
  - mmWave systems  
  - Satellite communication infrastructure  
  - Telecom export equipment  

- Programmable AC sources and frequency converters critical because telecom equipment must comply with:
  - US 60Hz standards  
  - European 50Hz systems  
  - Aircraft-grade 400Hz environments  

- India’s accelerating 5G rollout could structurally increase telecom-grade testing demand over the next several years  

---

**3. Industrial Automation & EV Ecosystem**
- Industrial automation and EV-related demand contribute around 12% share globally  
- Key growth areas include:
  - Robotics  
  - Servo drives  
  - Solar inverters  
  - EV battery systems  
  - Factory automation  

- EV battery testing becoming a major long-term demand driver for programmable DC systems used in:
  - Charge-discharge simulation  
  - Thermal stress testing  
  - Fast charging validation  
  - Motor controller testing  
  - BMS reliability testing  

- Industry 4.0 transition is steadily increasing requirement for precision testing infrastructure  

---

**4. Defence & Aerospace – Smallest Segment, Highest Moat**
- Defence and aerospace contribute only ~5% market share but often generate highest margins  
- Ruggedised programmable power systems used in:
  - Radar electronics  
  - Missile systems  
  - Avionics  
  - Military-grade testing  
  - Harsh industrial environments  

- Industry characteristics include:
  - Long approval cycles  
  - High qualification barriers  
  - Extremely low vendor substitution risk  
  - Sticky customer relationships  

- Specialized ruggedised systems reportedly among the fastest-growing high-value categories globally  

---

**5. Healthcare, Research Labs & Semiconductor Ecosystem**
- Another underappreciated demand layer includes:
  - MRI & CT testing systems  
  - Semiconductor characterization  
  - IIT/ISRO/CSIR lab infrastructure  
  - Medical device compliance systems  
  - Energy storage research  

- As India deepens focus on:
  - Semiconductor manufacturing  
  - Aerospace  
  - Electronics manufacturing  
  - Strategic R&D  

- Precision programmable power systems could become increasingly critical infrastructure  

---

**Industry Shift – Hardware to Intelligent Engineering Platforms**
- Industry rapidly evolving from conventional standalone power supplies toward:
  - Smart programmable systems  
  - IoT-connected architectures  
  - Remote-controlled infrastructure  
  - AI-monitored testing ecosystems  
  - Real-time telemetry & diagnostics  

- Programmable DC systems with remote monitoring already represent significant portion of

CITI on PB Fin

Buy, TP Rs 2275

Analyst day key takeaways,

(1) sustained market share accretion across most insurance segments backed by product innovation, vertical integration (ex. claims management, improving the underwriting journey of manufacturers, garage network, etc.) and ramp up of tech capabilities,

(2) focus on transitioning to an ecosystem-based approach for product distribution compared to traditional models focusing solely on insurance sales,

(3) driving higher business growth through phy-gital channels,

(4) investing in ramping up new business segments (ex. Pension Bazaar),

(5) multiple levers to contain any commission related challenges to the P&L owing to diversified business model and

(6) product co-creation with manufacturers to target untapped customer segments.

Arfin India Ltd | Q4 FY26 & FY26 Result Highlights

Arfin India reported a strong Q4 FY26 performance, led by better profitability and sharp margin expansion.

Q4 FY26 Performance
• Revenue from Operations: ₹19,323.76 lakhs
↳ Up 2.81% QoQ and 26.00% YoY

• EBITDA: ₹1,632.90 lakhs
↳ Up 17.58% QoQ and 147.98% YoY

↳ Improved by 106 bps QoQ and 416 bps YoY

↳ Up 34.04% QoQ and 1,180.60% YoY

FY26 vs FY25
• Revenue: ₹61,799.18 lakhs vs ₹61,575.38 lakhs — 0.36% growth
• EBITDA: ₹4,616.13 lakhs vs ₹3,630.01 lakhs — 27.17% growth
• EBITDA Margin: 7.47% vs 5.90% — up 157 bps
• PAT: ₹1,545.13 lakhs vs ₹914.63 lakhs — 68.93% growth

✅ Key takeaway:
Revenue remained largely stable on a full-year basis, but EBITDA and PAT grew strongly, showing better operating efficiency, improved margins and stronger earnings quality.

**COPPER – THE METAL AT THE CENTER OF THE NEXT INDUSTRIAL SUPER CYCLE**

**Why Copper Matters**
- Multiple global megatrends are converging on one critical metal:
  - AI infrastructure  
  - Electric vehicles  
  - Renewable energy  
  - Grid modernization  
  - Defence infrastructure  

- Copper remains extremely difficult to substitute due to:
  - High electrical conductivity  
  - Thermal efficiency  
  - Durability  
  - Reliability across industrial applications  

**1. AI Data Center Demand**
- Every 1 MW of AI data center capacity requires ~27 tonnes of copper  

- Copper heavily used across:
  - Power distribution systems  
  - Transformers  
  - Cooling systems  
  - Networking infrastructure  
  - High-density cabling  
  - Grid connectivity  

- Global data center copper demand projected to rise:
  - ~1.1 million MT (2025)  
  - ~2.5 million MT by 2040  

- AI training infrastructure could contribute ~58% of total data center copper demand by 2030  

- AI is increasingly becoming:
  - A power infrastructure story  
  - A grid expansion story  
  - A metals demand story  

**2. Electric Vehicles (EVs)**
- EVs use ~2.9x more copper than ICE vehicles  

- Copper required in:
  - Electric motors  
  - Battery systems  
  - Wiring harnesses  
  - Inverters  
  - Charging systems  
  - Power electronics  

- EV-related copper demand projected to increase:
  - ~1.7 million MT (2025)  
  - ~4.3 million MT by 2035  

- Additional copper demand also coming from:
  - EV charging networks  
  - Battery gigafactories  
  - Transmission infrastructure  

**3. Renewable Energy Transition**
- Renewable systems require ~2.5x–7x more copper versus fossil fuel systems  

- Copper is critical for:
  - Solar farms  
  - Wind turbines  
  - Inverters  
  - Transformers  
  - Battery energy storage  
  - Grid integration  

- AI-led power demand is also indirectly accelerating renewable infrastructure investments globally  

**4. Global Grid Modernization**
- IEA expects global electricity demand to rise ~50% by 2040  

- Massive investments expected in:
  - Transmission lines  
  - HVDC infrastructure  
  - Smart grids  
  - Substations  
  - Distribution modernization  
  - Electrification systems  

- Copper remains foundational across nearly every layer of modern power infrastructure  

**5. Defence & Strategic Infrastructure**
- Rising geopolitical tensions increasing defence spending globally  

- Copper demand rising across:
  - Advanced electronics  
  - Naval electrification  
  - Radar systems  
  - Aerospace systems  
  - Secure communications  
  - Military-grade power systems  

- Copper increasingly becoming a strategic sovereignty metal  

**The Real Challenge: Supply Constraints**
- New copper mines typically require 10–15+ years to develop  

- Structural supply risks include:
  - Declining ore grades  
  - Tougher permitting norms  
  - ESG constraints  
  - Geopolitical concentration risks  

- This creates possibility of long-duration structural supply tightness  

**India’s Copper Ecosystem**

- Precision Wires India Limited  
  - Leader in magnet wires  
  - Beneficiary of EV, transformer and renewable demand growth  

- Ram Ratna Wires Limited  
  - Exposure to wires, HVAC and EV ecosystem growth  

- Hindustan Copper Limited  
  - India’s only integrated copper miner  
  - Targeting ~3x capacity expansion by FY31  

- Hindalco Industries Limited  
  - Operates Birla Copper smelting business at Dahej  

- Adani Enterprises Limited  
  - Developing Kutch Copper project with large-scale smelting ambitions  

- Cubex Tubings Limited  
  - Niche copper tubes and rods player  

**Core Structural Theme**
- Copper is increasingly evolving from a cyclical industrial commodity into:
  - An AI infrastructure metal  
  - An electrification metal  
  - An energy transition metal  
  - A defence infrastructure metal  
  - A strategic resource for nations  

**Final Takeaway**
- The next decade’s infrastructure buildout across AI, EVs, renewables, grids and defence could create one of the strongest long-duration structural

CITI on Titan

Neutral, TP Rs 5075

Standalone jewellery revenue growth (ex-bullion) was strong at 45% YoY (2% above Citi est).

However, jewellery margin contracted by 135bps YoY to 10.5% (vs 11.5% Citi est); Domestic TMZ EBIT margin declined 60bps YoY to 11.3%.

Management highlighted:

(a) standalone margin had impact of Rs800mn for transfer pricing pertaining to sales to international subsidiaries;

(b) likely gained 50-60bps market share in FY26;

(c) led by gold price increase, there was some advancement of wedding purchases.

While near-term growth outlook remains strong, sustained increase in gold prices might have preponed consumer demand and to that extent growth in FY27E will be key to sustaining current valuations

JPM on BoB

OW TP Rs 335

4QFY26 core operating performance came better vs JPMe with broad-based beats across NIM, recoveries and opex.

NII/PPOP/PAT came 2%/11%/6% above JPMe respectively.

RAM (retail, agri and MSME) segments were the key growth drivers with 5.9%/6.3%/4.2% q/q growth respectively with share rising to 61% of domestic advances (vs 59.8% y/y) with growth focus likely to remain high in this segment for granular as well as NIM accretive growth.

Deposit growth (+12% y/y) lagged loan growth (+16.5% y/y).

However, BOB does not see this as a growth constraint and expects loan growth of 12-14% in FY27 (in line, JPMe 13.5%).

Jefferies on BoB

Hold, TP Rs 295

Q4 profit of Rs56bn, up 11% YoY, was ahead of est and included many non-core gains that were partly used to make floating provisions.

Loans grew by 16% and NII growth of 9% included some interest on tax refund.

Asset quality and core credit costs have been stable.

LDR of 83% and bulk deposit share at 23% of domestic deposit is higher than PSU peers.

Kotak Inst Eqt on BOB

ADD, TP Rs 300

BoB reported 10% yoy earnings growth, although several line items that are usually hard to forecast drove the better-than-expected performance.

NIM increased 10 bps qoq.

Slippages stood at 1%, with management comfortable that the Middle East exposure remains manageable at this stage.

Barring adverse macro-outcomes, management expressed confidence in raising FY2027 growth guidance while maintaining operating ratios unchanged.

CITI on MGL

Buy, TP Rs 1400

MGL reported 4Q EBITDA at Rs2.6bn (-26% qoq), in line with estimates.

While volumes were slightly ahead (+6% yoy), this was offset by slightly weaker-than-expected margins.

Reported net income at Rs1.3bn (-35% qoq) was also largely in line.

FY26 EPS was Rs86/sh (FY25: Rs105/sh).

MGL declared a final divi. of Rs18/sh (full year divi. of Rs30/sh).

While LNG supply disruptions linked to Middle East tensions continue to pose risks to near-term volumes & margins, recent gov’t policy initiatives remain supportive of longer-term CGD sector growth, keeping us positively inclined on stock

MS on Swiggy

EW,  TP Rs 322

Key highlights,

1) Slight relative market share gain based on GOV metrics of FD in 4Q,

2) QC growth in F27 likely to see a sharp deceleration,

3) Management maintains break even at contribution margin (CM) level in QC business in 1Q (MSe assumes 2QF27).

Kotak Inst Eqt on Swiggy

Buy, TP Rs 370

Swiggy’s food delivery GOV growth in 4Q came in at 23% yoy & ahead of estimates.

Instamart’s GOV grew 67% yoy but decelerated to flat growth qoq; NOV growth was a tad better at 3.6% qoq.

Instamart’s contribution margin improved from (-)2.5% in 3Q to (-)1.8% in 4Q, though absolute EBITDA loss remained high at Rs8.6 bn

Believe that Instamart may see further growth pressures & a scenario of weak GOV growth, and high absolute losses (CM break-even notwithstanding) may persist

Trim Instamart revenue and EBITDA estimates & ascribe a valuation of Rs69 bn (Rs111 bn earlier) to this business.

CLSA on BOB

O-P, TP Rs 335

4Q PBT in line but had several one-offs including interest on IT refund, employee provision reversals and floating provisions.

Core performance was mixed.

Loan growth picked up to 16.5% YoY & better than that of many banks.

Deposit growth marginally picked up to 12% YoY, while CASA ratio improved slightly QoQ.

While reported NIM improved 10bp QoQ, believe it was driven by interest on IT refund.
Core NIM, would have declined.

Loan yields declined 12bp QoQ due to the Dec 2025 repo rate cut, while cost of deposits hardened by 3bp.

Fee income declined YoY despite healthy loan growth performance.

Management has guided to 2.75-2.95% NIMs in FY27 and expects similar interest on IT refunds through FY27 as well.

CITI on BoB

Buy, TP Rs 340

4Q PAT of Rs56.2bn (RoA: 1.15%) was materially aided by one-offs —IT refund of Rs12.9bn, recoveries from written-off accounts of Rs14.85bn, dividend income of Rs2.9bn, and a Rs2.2bn reversal of retirement benefit provisions.

Reported NIMs expanded 10bps QoQ to 2.89%, though was largely IT-refund-driven; stripping this out, YoAs contracted ~12bps while CoDs crept up 3bps — underscoring persistent core NIM pressure

Bank proactively created Rs15bn of floating provisions as a buffer against global macro headwinds.

Management revised FY27 loan growth guidance upward to 12–14% (vs. 11–13% prior), with NIMs guided at 2.75–2.95% (incl benefit of int on IT refund), credit cost below 60bps, and RoA above 1%.

Bernstein on SBI

Market Perform, TP Rs 1300

SBI delivered a mixed 4Q26

Loan growth accelerated to 17% YoY but NIM saw a sharp 18 bps QoQ decline to 2.93%, steepest compression amongst large banks.

Slippages rose sequentially, though largely seasonal, while headline GNPA improved to 1.49%.

RoA moderated 12 bps QoQ to 1.07%, weighed down by NIM compression & a Treasury MTM loss.

Management retained FY27 guidance of 13–15% credit growth, >3% domestic NIM & 50 bps credit costs

Management reaffirmed its through-cycle commitment to 1%+ RoA & 15%+ RoE

Outflows Fade, But Re-entry Waits

Foreign ownership has retreated to historical lows, below DIIs

Petroleum coke - could a little-known raw material be another big supply risk for aluminium? Good Read From JPM

The global aluminium market faces heightened supply risks due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting both alumina and petroleum coke (pet coke) supplies. 

Pet coke, a critical raw material for carbon anodes in aluminium smelting, has seen only a +21% price increase despite ~20% of its supply being impacted by the conflict. 

While no major shortages have been reported yet, producers like Alcoa and Norsk Hydro note rising carbon-related costs and supply uncertainty. 

The aluminium industry remains dependent on pet coke in the near term, as inert anode technologies (e.g., ELYSIS, HalZero) are still in development.

Sathlokhar Synergys E&C Global Limited Receives Credit Ratings from CRISIL Ratings Limited

Sathlokhar Synergys E&C Global Limited has received credit ratings from CRISIL Ratings Limited for its ₹200 Cr bank loan facilities, reflecting a strengthening in its overall business and financial profile.

Rating Summary:

Long Term Rating: CRISIL BBB+/Positive (Assigned)
Short Term Rating: CRISIL A2 (Assigned)
Total Facilities Rated: ₹200 Cr

The Positive outlook underscores the Company’s improved operating performance, marked by robust revenue growth, healthy margins, and strong debt protection metrics. The Company achieved a significant scale up during FY26, reporting revenue of approximately ₹800 Cr, driven by repeat business from existing clients and the addition of new customers.

The ratings also take into account the Company’s experienced management team, diversified EPC presence across multiple sectors, and a strong order book that provides healthy revenue visibility. Its financial profile remains
